title: Brazilian-Style Lamb Skewers
description: A classic Brazilian dish featuring marinated lamb skewers grilled to perfection, served with a side of fresh vegetables.

ingredients.name: Lamb shoulder, cut into 1-inch cubes
ingredients.notes: You can also use a mix of lamb and beef for added flavor

ingredients.name: Fresh lime juice
ingredients.notes: Use freshly squeezed lime juice for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Brazilian churrasco seasoning
ingredients.notes: You can also use a mix of cumin, coriander, and chili powder as a substitute

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality olive oil for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: Use fresh garlic for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Onion, thinly sliced
ingredients.notes: You can also use a mix of onion and bell pepper for added flavor

ingredients.name: Bell pepper, thinly sliced
ingredients.notes: You can also use a mix of onion and bell pepper for added flavor

ingredients.name: Zucchini, sliced
ingredients.notes: You can also use other summer squash as a substitute

ingredients.name: Tomato, halved
ingredients.notes: Use fresh, ripe tomatoes for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Fresh cilantro, chopped
ingredients.notes: Use fresh cilantro for the best flavor

instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together lime juice, churrasco seasoning, olive oil, garlic, and onion.
Add the lamb cubes to the marinade and mix well to coat.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Thread the marinated lamb, bell pepper, zucchini, and tomato onto skewers.
Grill the skewers for 8-10 minutes per side, or until the lamb is cooked to your desired level of doneness.
Serve the skewers hot, garnished with chopped cilantro and a side of fresh vegetables.
